title: Verification harness enforcing the HARD RULE: dual-backend bit-compare + BBP spot-check + known-reference check + guard-digit report
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The HARD RULE binds all computation-run and release work: no digit is accepted or published unless two independent computations are bit-identical, BBP/Bellard hex spot-checks pass, every chunk is recompute-verified and hashed, the published prefix matches a known reference, and the guard-digit analysis is recorded; an author may not verify their own run. This harness mechanizes that gate and is the spine of the project's verification-first posture; it depends on both core-002 and core-003.
objective: Build the verification harness that enforces the HARD RULE: second-backend bit-identical re-derivation, BBP/Bellard hex spot-checks, known-reference cross-check, and a guard-digit report emitting a VerificationRecord.
acceptanceCriteria: Re-derives a result with a second independent bignum backend and asserts bit-identical (excluding the declared G guard digits); fails loudly on any mismatch.
Runs >= 64 random BBP/Bellard hex spot-checks plus the hex tail against the assembled binary; all must pass; the residual-error probability bound is documented.
Cross-checks the published prefix against a known reference value.
Emits a VerificationRecord (hashes, backends, spot-check results, guard digits, verifier id); refuses to mark 'verified' unless every limb passes.
An author cannot sign off their own run (role-separation enforced in process and record fields).
output: A PR adding the verification harness that enforces the HARD RULE and emits a VerificationRecord.
